stem: A large water jug holds 2.5 liters. There are 1,000 milliliters in 1 liter. How many milliliters does the water jug hold?
explanation:
To convert liters to milliliters, multiply the number of liters by 1,000 milliliters per liter.

$$2.5 \text{ L} \times \frac{1{,}000 \text{ mL}}{1 \text{ L}} = 2{,}500 \text{ mL}$$

The liters unit cancels, leaving milliliters. The water jug holds 2,500 milliliters.

distractorRationale.A: Wrong conversion factor: multiplies by 100 instead of 1,000, giving $2.5 \times 100 = 250$ mL. The student confuses the prefix milli- (meaning 1/1,000) with centi- (meaning 1/100), using the centiliter conversion factor instead.
distractorRationale.B: Inverted conversion: divides 1,000 by 2.5 instead of multiplying 2.5 by 1,000, giving $1{,}000 \div 2.5 = 400$ mL. The student applies the conversion factor in the wrong direction.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong conversion factor: multiplies by 10,000 instead of 1,000, giving $2.5 \times 10{,}000 = 25{,}000$ mL. The student misremembers the conversion as 10,000 milliliters per liter rather than 1,000.
